popularStory.headline: From Lyman Place genealogical complilation
popularStory.description:
"WALTER STONE (ATTA) b. AB.1390 LIVED IN THE REIGN OF RICHARD II (1377-1399), HENRY VII (1399-1413), HENRY VI (1413-1422) AND HENRY VI (1422-1461)  HE d. AFTER 1431
WIFES NAME NOT KNOWN"

zh.lifeSummary: 當Walter Atte Stone於1390年出生在英國英格蘭艾可阿爾德利時，他的父親William atte Stone25歲，母親Mrs. William Atte Stone21歲。他於大約1415年與Elizabeth Empson在英國艾可結婚。他們至少是1個兒子的父母。他去世於1431年6月2日在他的家鄉享年41歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭艾可阿爾德利。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Walter Atte Stone于1390年出生在英国英格兰艾可阿爾德利时，他的父亲William atte Stone25岁，母亲Mrs. William Atte Stone21岁。他于大约1415年与Elizabeth Empson在英国艾可结婚。他们至少是1个儿子的父母。他去世于1431年6月2日在他的家乡享年41岁，埋葬在英国英格兰艾可阿爾德利。

nameMeanings.name: Atte
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Muslim (mainly Pakistan and Egypt): variant of   Ata  .
West African (Ghana): from an Akan (Fante) male personal name denoting a twin. Compare   Arthur  .

variants.name: Ata
meanings: Turkish: ornamental name, nickname, or relationship name from ata ‘father, elder’.
Muslim (mainly Egypt): from a personal name based on Arabic ʿaṭā ‘gift’ (understood as ‘gift from God’). Compare Atallah and Atta .

nameMeanings.name: Stone
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone, rock’ (Old English stān). The surname may be topographic, for someone who lived on stony ground, by a notable outcrop of rock, or by a stone boundary-marker or monument, or habitational, from a place called Stone, such as those in Buckinghamshire, Devon, Gloucestershire, Hampshire, Kent, Somerset, Staffordshire, and Worcestershire.
Irish (Kilkenny): adopted for Irish Ó Clochartaigh (see Clougherty ) and/or Ó Clochasaigh (see Clohessy ), and possibly several other names containing or thought to contain the element cloch ‘stone’.
Americanized form (translation into English) of various surnames in other languages, meaning ‘stone’, including Jewish Stein , Norwegian Steine, French Lapierre .

variants.name: Stoner
meanings: English (Sussex): topographic name from Middle English ston(e) ‘stone’ + -er, used for someone who lived by a prominent rock or stone, or perhaps who came from a place called Stone, although this formation is more rare. See Stone and compare Stoneman .
Americanized form (translation into English) of German   Steiner  .

variants.name: Stones
meanings: English (Yorkshire and Lancashire): variant of Stone with plural or post-medieval excrescent -s. A number of the places called Stone are sometimes recorded with plural spellings in the medieval period, including Stone (Staffordshire), recorded as Stanes in 1187.

variants.name: Stenner
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Hans, Kurt.

German: occupational name for a stonemason or stonecutter, or a topographic name for someone who lived on stony ground, from a derivative of Middle High German stein ‘stone’, ‘stony place’. Compare Stone and Stein .
English: variant of   Stayner  .

variants.name: Clougherty
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Aileen.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Clochartaigh ‘descendant of Clochartach’, a personal name, probably formed with cloch ‘stone’, Stone being an alternative Anglicized form of the surname.

variants.name: Roach
meanings: English:

of Norman origin, from Old French and Middle English roche ‘rock, cliff, promontory’, sometimes a translation of Stone . The surname may have been topographic, denoting someone who lived on or by a prominent rock or rocky place, or a habitational name for someone who lived or came from a place so named, such as one of those called Roque or Roche(s) in Normandy and neighboring regions, or Roche in Cornwall, Roach Farm in Clyst Hydon (Devon), or Roch in Pembrokeshire (see Roch ).

in Lancashire perhaps referring to the River Roch, which runs through Rochdale; the river name is probably a back-formation from Recedham, an early name for Rochdale recorded in 1086, derived from Old English reced ‘building, house, hall’ + hām ‘village, homestead’. Alternatively, the Lancashire name could be a shortened form of the now rare or extinct surname Rochdale alias Rachedale, Rachdall, a habitational name from Rochdale (Lancashire). From the 18th century onward the name is difficult to distinguish from Roch , borne by Irish migrants to South Lancashire, especially Liverpool.

variants.name: Stoneman
meanings: English: perhaps an occupational name for someone who was the servant (Middle English man) of a person surnamed Stone . Alternatively, the name may have been an alias of Stone , used for someone who lived by a prominent stone or rock or at a minor place so named (Middle English ston + man).
English: occasionally perhaps an occupational name for someone who worked with stone or who sold precious stones; a jeweler.

English: perhaps from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Ston(e)man (Old English Stān(e)mann), a pet form of Old English personal names beginning with Stān- (from the word ‘stone’, ‘rock’) + the Old English hypocoristic suffix -mann.

nameMeanings.name: Walter
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old French personal name of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from wald ‘rule’ + heri, hari ‘army’. This was adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to England, superseding the native Old English form, Wealdhere. It was a very popular name in medieval England, normally pronounced ‘Water’.

variants.name: Walther
meanings: from wald ‘rule’ + heri ‘army’; name of the famous medieval minnesinger Walther von der Vogelweide ( c.1170– c.1230 ).

en.lifeSummary: When Walter Atte Stone was born in 1390, in Ardleigh, Essex, England, United Kingdom, his father, William atte Stone, was 25 and his mother, Mrs. William Atte Stone, was 21. He married Elizabeth Empson about 1415, in Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 1 son. He died on 2 June 1431, in his hometown, at the age of 41, and was buried in Ardleigh, Essex, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Walter Atte Stone est né en 1390, à Ardleigh, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, William atte Stone, avait 25 ans et sa mère, Mrs. William Atte Stone, avait 21 ans. Il a épousé Elizabeth Empson environ 1415, à Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Il est décédé le 2 juin 1431, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 41 ans, et a été enterré à Ardleigh, Essex, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Walter Atte Stone 1390 in Ardleigh, Essex, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater William atte Stone 25 und seine Mutter Mrs. William Atte Stone 21. Er hat um 1415 in Essex, England Elizabeth Empson geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 2. Juni 1431 im Alter von 41 Jahren in seinem Geburtsort und wurde in Ardleigh, Essex, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Atte Stone è nato il nel 1390 nel Ardleigh, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, William atte Stone, aveva 25 anni e il suo madre, Mrs. William Atte Stone, aveva 21 anni. Lui sposato Elizabeth Empson circa 1415, nel Essex, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio. Morì il 2 giugno 1431, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 41 anni, e fu sepolto a Ardleigh, Essex, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Walter Atte Stoneが1390年にイギリスイギリスエセックスアルドレイグで生まれた時，父William atte Stoneは25歳，母Mrs. William Atte Stoneは21歳であった。約1415年にイギリスエセックスでElizabeth Empsonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子の親であった。彼は1431年6月2日に故郷で41歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスエセックスアルドレイグにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Walter Atte Stone(이)가 1390년 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 아들리에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William atte Stone(은)는 25세였고, 그의 어머니 Mrs. William Atte Stone(은)는 21세였다. 그는 대략 1415년 영국 에세스에서 Elizabeth Empson(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1431년 6월 2일 그의 고향에서 41세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 에세스 아들리에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Walter Atte Stone nasceu em 1390, em Ardleigh, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, William atte Stone, tinha 25 anos e sua mãe, Mrs. William Atte Stone, tinha 21 anos. Ele casou-se com Elizabeth Empson aproximadamente 1415, em Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho. Ele faleceu em 2 de junho de 1431, em sua cidade natal, com 41 anos, e foi sepultado em Ardleigh, Essex, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Walter Atte Stone родился в 1390 г. в местности ардлиг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени William atte Stone было 25 лет, а матери, которую звали Mrs. William Atte Stone, 21. Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Empson около 1415 г. в местности эссекс, Англия. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын. Он умер 2 июня 1431 г. в его родном городе в возрасте 41 год и был похоронен в местности ардлиг, эссекс, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Walter Atte Stone nació en 1390, en Ardleigh, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, William atte Stone, tenía 25 años y su mamá, Mrs. William Atte Stone, tenía 21 años. Se casó con Elizabeth Empson aproximadamente 1415, en Essex, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hijo. Murió el 2 de junio de 1431, en su pueblo, a la edad de 41 años, y fue sepultado en Ardleigh, Essex, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
